subjects.heroSentence: This account won 20 of 48 long-shot bets that the market gave roughly a 12 percent chance. By luck you would expect about 5.76 wins. A record this strong is consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~0h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~12% across 50 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: chance of ≥ 20 wins in 48 tries at 12% each
scorecard.numbers: Won 20 of 48 independent bets the market priced at ~12% — about 5.76 expected by luck. Probability by chance ≈ 1 in 4.5M (2 correlated bets de-correlated).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $3,787 is ~38× this wallet's median bet ($100) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: winning long-shots bought within hours of resolution
scorecard.numbers: 2 of 8 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 5h before) — entering a ~19% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

subjects.heroSentence: This account won 149 of 224 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 54 percent — roughly 120.39 expected by luck (4.56 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 400K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 149 of 224 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~54% — about 120.4 expected by luck (4.6σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 400K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − market mean) ÷ market SD vs peers
scorecard.numbers: Profited 2.4 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 45 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

subjects.confidenceLimiter: common-ownership is inferred from funding heuristics, not confirmed identity

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 103 of 116 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 78 percent — roughly 90.56 expected by luck (3.51 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 4.5K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 103 of 116 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~78% — about 90.6 expected by luck (3.5σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 4.5K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~15% across 14 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~0h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − market mean) ÷ market SD vs peers
scorecard.numbers: Profited 4.4 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: winning bets bought right before the price-shock
scorecard.numbers: 1 of 2 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 26h before) — entering a ~16% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 9 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $21,527 is ~19× this wallet's median bet ($1,109) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the cross-category test uses a normal approximation over mixed odds; it ranks how improbable the record is, it does not prove intent

subjects.heroSentence: These 4 wallets appear to be controlled by a single entity (shared on-chain funding + co-ordinated betting). Together they won 50 of 262 long-shot bets the market gave about a 11 percent chance — the pattern you would expect from foreknowledge split across wallets, not luck. Consistent with informed trading by one operator, not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~11% across 289 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: chance of ≥ 50 wins in 262 tries at 11% each
scorecard.numbers: Won 50 of 262 independent bets the market priced at ~11% — about 28.98 expected by luck. Probability by chance ≈ 1 in 10.9K (27 correlated bets de-correlated).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $5,896 is ~421× this wallet's median bet ($14) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: winning long-shots bought within hours of resolution
scorecard.numbers: 6 of 21 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 0h before) — entering a ~6% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 180 of 302 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 51 percent — roughly 154.26 expected by luck (4.13 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 54.1K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 180 of 302 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~51% — about 154.3 expected by luck (4.1σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 54.1K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~11% across 147 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~5h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 51 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $8,040 is ~82× this wallet's median bet ($98) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the cross-category test uses a normal approximation over mixed odds; it ranks how improbable the record is, it does not prove intent

subjects.improbCaption: composite suspicion score
subjects.heroSentence: This account's most anomalous (wallet, market) episode scores 1004 on the Harvard composite — it bet $64K at about 89% on “Will GreenLeft–Labour win the most seats in the 2025 Netherlands parli”, and won. The score combines all five signals — out-profiting the market, an outsized bet vs peers, an outsized bet vs its own norm, buying late (final 48h), and one-sided conviction — weighted as in Ofir & Ofir (profit/size dominate; timing and directionality are minor). We additionally retain only episodes that actually won and out-profited the market. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 30
scorecard.numbers: You netted $8K on this market; the average trader there made $-14 ± $528 — 14.25 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 25
scorecard.numbers: You staked $64K here; the average trader staked $466 ± $4K — 17.61 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_wallet) / σ_wallet · weight 20
scorecard.numbers: You staked $64K here vs your own usual bet — 6.29 SD above your norm.

scorecard.formula: late_buy_fraction (share of buys in the final 48h) · weight 15
scorecard.numbers: 0% of buying was in the final 48h before resolution

scorecard.formula: directional_score (1 − sold/bought) · weight 10
scorecard.numbers: 100% one-directional (held, not hedged)

scorecard.formula: z = (this wallet's profit − market mean) ÷ market SD, over peers in the SAME market
scorecard.numbers: Profited 14.2 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~0h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $434,785 is ~1112× this wallet's median bet ($391) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: max(YES, NO stake) ÷ total staked
scorecard.numbers: 96% of $625,480 staked one direction (NO), 70% in a single event cluster — the un-hedged, single-thesis concentration of someone betting what they already know.

subjects.heroSentence: This account placed a single $24K bet at roughly 16 percent on “usisrael-strikes-iran-by” and cashed out about $214K. One bet is not statistically improbable on its own — but a lone, outsized, high-conviction long-shot like this, alongside the other signals, is the single-bet informed-trading signature. Consistent with informed trading, not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~13% across 56 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: one large long-shot win held to resolution
scorecard.numbers: a single $23,699 bet at ~11% that paid ~$214,405 — a high-conviction long-shot concentrated on one outcome, the single-event insider signature (the binomial needs ≥5 independent events; this is the confluence path).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $591,043 is ~90× this wallet's median bet ($6,555) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: winning long-shots bought within hours of resolution
scorecard.numbers: 2 of 3 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 2d before) — entering a ~10% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: z = (this wallet's profit − market mean) ÷ market SD, over peers in the SAME market
scorecard.numbers: Profited 3.6 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 129 of 213 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 51 percent — roughly 108.09 expected by luck (3.43 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 3.4K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 129 of 213 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~51% — about 108.1 expected by luck (3.4σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 3.4K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~18% across 77 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~2h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 33 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $33,622 is ~76× this wallet's median bet ($442) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the cross-category test uses a normal approximation over mixed odds; it ranks how improbable the record is, it does not prove intent

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 88 of 175 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 41 percent — roughly 72.25 expected by luck (3.01 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 757). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 88 of 175 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~41% — about 72.3 expected by luck (3.0σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 757). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~14% across 88 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: winning bets bought right before the price-shock
scorecard.numbers: 1 of 3 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 6h before) — entering a ~12% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 37 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $17,684 is ~111× this wallet's median bet ($159) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the cross-category test uses a normal approximation over mixed odds; it ranks how improbable the record is, it does not prove intent

subjects.improbCaption: composite suspicion score
subjects.heroSentence: This account's most anomalous (wallet, market) episode scores 859 on the Harvard composite — it bet $13K at about 55% on “Will SpaceX IPO by June 30, 2026?”, and won. The score combines all five signals — out-profiting the market, an outsized bet vs peers, an outsized bet vs its own norm, buying late (final 48h), and one-sided conviction — weighted as in Ofir & Ofir (profit/size dominate; timing and directionality are minor). We additionally retain only episodes that actually won and out-profited the market. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 30
scorecard.numbers: You netted $10K on this market; the average trader there made $31 ± $737 — 13.65 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 25
scorecard.numbers: You staked $13K here; the average trader staked $121 ± $711 — 17.47 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_wallet) / σ_wallet · weight 20
scorecard.numbers: You staked $13K here vs your own usual bet — 0.15 SD above your norm.

scorecard.formula: late_buy_fraction (share of buys in the final 48h) · weight 15
scorecard.numbers: 0% of buying was in the final 48h before resolution

scorecard.formula: directional_score (1 − sold/bought) · weight 10
scorecard.numbers: 99% one-directional (held, not hedged)

scorecard.formula: z = (this wallet's profit − market mean) ÷ market SD, over peers in the SAME market
scorecard.numbers: Profited 13.7 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: count of distinct surprising events the wallet was early-and-right on
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 3 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~9h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $1,292,554 is ~4427× this wallet's median bet ($292) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

One bet is not statistically improbable on its own — but a lone, outsized, high-conviction long-shot like this, alongside the other signals, is the single-bet informed-trading signature. Consistent with informed trading, not proof of it.

subjects.improbCaption: composite suspicion score
subjects.heroSentence: This account's most anomalous (wallet, market) episode scores 583 on the Harvard composite — it bet $26K at about 89% on “MicroStrategy sells any Bitcoin by December 31, 2026?”, and won. The score combines all five signals — out-profiting the market, an outsized bet vs peers, an outsized bet vs its own norm, buying late (final 48h), and one-sided conviction — weighted as in Ofir & Ofir (profit/size dominate; timing and directionality are minor). We additionally retain only episodes that actually won and out-profited the market. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 30
scorecard.numbers: You netted $3K on this market; the average trader there made $-1 ± $434 — 7.16 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_market) / σ_market · weight 25
scorecard.numbers: You staked $26K here; the average trader staked $266 ± $2K — 13.95 SD above.

scorecard.formula: z = (stake − μ_wallet) / σ_wallet · weight 20
scorecard.numbers: You staked $26K here vs your own usual bet — -0.04 SD above your norm.

scorecard.formula: late_buy_fraction (share of buys in the final 48h) · weight 15
scorecard.numbers: 69% of buying was in the final 48h before resolution

scorecard.formula: directional_score (1 − sold/bought) · weight 10
scorecard.numbers: 100% one-directional (held, not hedged)

scorecard.formula: z = (this wallet's profit − market mean) ÷ market SD, over peers in the SAME market
scorecard.numbers: Profited 7.2 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: count of distinct surprising events the wallet was early-and-right on
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 4 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $2,293,157 is ~1680× this wallet's median bet ($1,365) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

permutation.statement: Is this just luck? We took every long-shot bet placed by the 40 flagged wallets — 4,492 bets in all — and used the market's own prices to work out how many they should win by pure guessing: about 2,478. They actually won 2,923. We then re-ran every outcome 10,000 times at random; the group beat chance by 17.8 standard deviations (p < 1e-4) — a margin so large it essentially never happens by accident. Strong evidence the group as a whole was trading on information; not proof for any single wallet.
